setup.delete_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete shooter "{{name}}"?

archive.title: Session Archive
archive.delete_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete this session?

analytics.pti_description: Performance Transfer Index shows how well training performance transfers into competition. Higher index indicates better transfer.

volatility.description: Volatility Index measures instability within the match. Lower values indicate higher performance stability.

analytics.gap_description: Competition Gap shows the difference between competition and training averages. Negative value indicates performance drop in competition, positive value indicates better performance under pressure.

insight.recovery_slow: Recovery after error is slower than optimal (Recovery {{value}}).
insight.recovery_stable: Mental reset after error is stable without performance spreading.
insight.recovery_partial: Reset after error is stable (success rate {{value}}%), but occasionally requires more than one shot for full stabilization.
insight.cascade_strong: In {{value}}% of drops, performance expands into a prolonged weaker sequence.
insight.cascade_moderate: In {{value}}% of cases, performance drop escalates into a mini‑series.
insight.cascade_mild: A recurring cascade pattern is present ({{value}}%).
insight.tempo_fast_strong: Tempo shows significant acceleration after error (Z = {{value}}).
insight.tempo_slow_strong: Tempo shows significant prolongation after error (Z = {{value}}).
insight.fatigue_strong_decline: Strong performance decline in the final phase (drift {{value}}).
insight.fatigue_mild_decline: Mild performance decline in the second half of the match (drift {{value}}).
insight.fatigue_strong_growth: Performance significantly improves in the closing phase (drift {{value}}).
insight.fatigue_mild_growth: A slight performance increase is observed in the second half (drift {{value}}).
insight.focus_mild_instability: Mild focus oscillations within shorter sequences (rolling std {{value}}).

recommendation.recovery_protocol: In {{percent}}% of situations, reset requires more than one shot. Introduce a mandatory 1–2 second mental pause after a sub‑threshold shot before raising the rifle again.
recommendation.recovery_maintain: Maintain the current reset routine and periodically test it under controlled conditions with intentional errors.
recommendation.recovery_shorten: Work on shortening the mental interval between error and next shot to reduce reset to a single attempt.
recommendation.cascade_interrupt: Introduce a mandatory reset protocol immediately after the first weaker shot (break the sequence, lower the rifle, apply a verbal reset cue).
recommendation.cascade_simulation: Simulate intentional errors during training and practice returning to stable mode with the very next shot.
recommendation.cascade_reaction_speed: Focus on reducing reaction time after an error without altering tempo or technique.
recommendation.tempo_pause_after_error: Introduce a mandatory 1–2 second pause after error before raising the rifle again to prevent impulsive reactions.
recommendation.tempo_breathing_control: Stabilize breathing rhythm and maintain standard aiming time without accelerating the next shot.
recommendation.tempo_automation: Reinforce automatism without excessively prolonging aiming after an error.
recommendation.tempo_rhythm_consistency: Maintain the same temporal pattern as in stable series and avoid over‑analysis of the next shot.
recommendation.fatigue_finish_training: Introduce extended series with simulated closing pressure and tempo control during the last 15 shots.
recommendation.fatigue_rhythm_stability: Focus training on maintaining stable rhythm and mental routine in the final phase of the match.
recommendation.fatigue_early_structure: Analyze match opening structure to establish stable rhythm earlier.
recommendation.fatigue_adaptive_start: Consider shorter adaptive routines at the beginning of the match to ensure early stability.
recommendation.focus_strict_routine: Implement strict repetition of identical mental and physical routine before every shot to reduce internal fluctuations.
recommendation.focus_stabilization: Work on stabilizing attention within series without changing technique or tempo.
recommendation.focus_continuity: Maintain current routine with additional emphasis on continuity between consecutive shots.
recommendation.maintain_regime: Maintain the current training regime with periodic review of mental patterns.

composite.stable_profile: The overall mental profile indicates stability without dominant weaknesses. Error response, tempo and closing phase are balanced.
composite.dominant_recovery: The dominant pattern relates to prolonged mental reset after errors, potentially disrupting shot continuity. Immediate stabilization after a drop should be prioritized.
composite.dominant_cascade: The dominant pattern is cascading performance decline following an initial mistake, indicating emotional inertia during the match. Focus should be on breaking the sequence with the very next shot.
composite.dominant_tempo: Tempo reaction after errors shows significant deviation from a stable pattern. Controlling rhythm and breathing after a drop can significantly stabilize performance.
composite.dominant_fatigue: The final phase of the match shows performance decline potentially linked to mental or physical fatigue. Maintaining stability in the closing segment should be a training priority.
composite.dominant_focus: Noticeable micro‑oscillations in focus within shorter sequences may affect shot continuity. Reinforcing stability of the mental routine within series is recommended.

technical.title: Technical Analysis (Mean Radius)
technical.correlation_desc: Correlation shows how technical stability (Mean Radius) relates to performance. Strong negative correlation means tighter grouping directly improves result.
technical.mean_radius_desc: Mean Radius trend indicates whether the shot group is tightening or expanding over time. Negative trend suggests technical improvement.

timing.title: Shot Timing Analysis
timing.pearson_description: Pearson correlation shows the relationship between shot time and result.

pearson_direction.positive: Longer aiming is associated with better results.
pearson_direction.negative: Longer aiming is associated with performance decline.
pearson_direction.neutral: Shot time has no clear impact on result.

mental.title: Mental Performance
mental.recovery_training_desc: Recovery Score measures how quickly the shooter recovers after dropping below the dynamic threshold.
mental.recovery_qualification_desc: Recovery Score measures mental recovery capability during competition.
mental.cascade_desc: Cascade Index shows percentage of performance drops followed by consecutive weaker shots.
mental.fatigue_desc: Fatigue Drift shows performance change between match halves.

closing.title: Closing Performance
closing.description: Closing Performance measures the difference between the first 15 and last 15 shots. Positive value indicates stronger finish, negative value indicates decline in the closing segment.

focus.description: Rolling standard deviation (5-shot window) measuring micro focus stability throughout the match.
